name: M.ImputedValue_WOCF
label: Worst Observation Carried Forward
description: Uses the worst (most extreme) observed value within a partition as the imputed value. Worst is defined by the direction configuration: 'high' takes the maximum, 'low' takes the minimum.

formula.notes: WORST is an alias for MAX when direction='high' (e.g., adverse events, lab toxicity) or MIN when direction='low' (e.g., efficacy scores where lower is worse). Only non-missing observed values up to the current time are considered.

configurations.name: direction
configurations.description: Which direction is 'worst': 'high' = MAX is worst (e.g., toxicity), 'low' = MIN is worst (e.g., efficacy where higher is better)

configurations.name: scope
configurations.description: Whether to consider only values observed up to the current time point (cumulative) or all observed values in the partition (all_observed)

methodInput.name: value
methodInput.description: Source values from which the worst is selected

assumptions: At least one non-missing observed value exists in the partition
The direction of 'worst' is clinically defined for the endpoint
